title: Fermented Tender Slovakian Oxtail Stew
description: A cozy, fermented stew with tender oxtail, caramelized vegetables, and a splash of traditional Slovakian kvass.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the sliced carrots and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, and smoked paprika; cook for 2 minutes.
Add the oxtail pieces, coating them in the vegetable mixture.
Pour in the kvass and vegetable broth; bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat, add bay leaves, caraway seeds, salt and pepper, then cover and simmer on low heat for 3 hours, or until the oxtail is fall‑apart tender.
Check occasionally, adding a bit of water if the liquid reduces too much.
Remove the bay leaves, taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley or a dollop of sour cream.
